calendar.matters:
NOTICE OF HEARING ON PETITION TO COMPEL THIRD PARTY DISCOVERY RESPONSES AS TO RESPONDENT HELLMAN & FRIEDMAN LLC (ADDED FOR POSTING OF TENTATIVE RULING)
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Third Party Discovery Responses As To Respondent Hellman & Friedman Llc
Off Calendar Jan-23-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PETITIONERS SRP MANAGEMENT SERVICES, LLC, STRONGHOLD RESOURCE PARTNERS, LLC's Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Third Party Discovery Responses As To Respondent Hellman & Friedman Llc.

Appearances (via Zoom):

JENNIFER RAPPOPORT, Esq., of BOTKIN CHIARELLO CALAF PLLC, (512) 960-4162, for Petitioners.

STEPHEN BLAKE, Esq., of SIMPSON THACHER & BARTLETT LLP, (650) 251-5000, for Respondent.

Petitioner and Respondent stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem Judge. The pro tem Judge, Petitioner, and Respondent (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

The matter is argued. At conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PETIONERS SRP MANAGEMENT SERVICES,LLC AND STRONGHOLD RESOURE PARTNERS, LLC'S AND (HEREAFTER EITHER "PETITIONERS" OR COLLECTIVELY "SRP") MOTION SEEKING TO COMPEL THIRD PARTY, HELLMAN & FRIEDMAN (HEREAFTER "HELLMAN"} TO RESPOND TO SRP'S SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS DATED 10/11/2023 IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY AND UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES IS UNDULY BURDEN TO THIRD PARTY. THE SCHEDULING ORDER ISSUED BY THE TRIAL COURT SPECIFICALLY PROVIDED FOR COMPLETION OF DISCOVERY BY DECEMBER 8, 2023 AND SET A TRIAL DATE FOR JANUARY 29, 2024. BASED ON THE TERMS OF THE SCHEDULING ORDER THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT ANY DOCUMENTS PRODUCED AT THIS LATE DATE WOULD BE ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE. A THIRD PARTY SHOULD NOT BE BURDENED WITH THIS SWEEPING DEMAND FOR DOCUMENTS WHERE THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL LIKELIHOOOD THAT IT WILL BE A FUTURE EXERCISE. COUNSEL FOR HELLMAN SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR PETITIONERS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar@gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON 1/23/24 REGARDLESS WHETHER PETITIONERS HAVE INDICATED THE APPROVAL.

The Judge Pro Tem confirms receipt of a proposed order and advises that he intends to execute it. No objection to the preceding is presented.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported (302/JPT)
